narrative.summary: Experimental jazz + spoken word in a narrow, dimly lit room. The most cutting-edge jazz spot in Toronto — next-gen artists, eclectic programming. 4.4★ (876 reviews). $$

blocks.title: The Space
blocks.body: You're on Queen West, past the chain shops, where the street still has some edge. Inside, it's a narrow room with dim lighting that leads to a back performance space—the kind of place where the stage feels close enough to see the spit valves empty. The entrance fee ($10-15) gets you past the front bar into that back room where experimental jazz and spoken word happen inches from your drink.

blocks.title: The Food
blocks.body: There isn't any. This is a listening room with a drinks menu—beer, wine, cocktails at reasonable prices for a venue that actually pays musicians. You're here for the next-gen jazz artists and unscripted collaborations, not dinner. Grab food before you come or after the set ends.

blocks.title: Go When
blocks.body: Go on a night when you actually want to hear something you've never heard before—smash jams, experimental sets, spoken word that doesn't play it safe. This isn't background music for conversation; it's a proper listening room where the programming takes risks.

blocks.title: The Vibe
blocks.body:
⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ "Had a fantastic time coming here for a jazz night. Inspiring music and stories.  Owner Shawn was great to give a tour of the back room as well. Has a drinks menu. No food. Looking forward to coming back. $15 entrance fee."

⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ "Funky place for Jazz! I was here for a smash jam of Local Jazz artists that decided to collaborate together unscripted! Super fun and different. Great service too and alcohol price was reasonable. $10 dollars entry fee to the back stage room."
